mnemonics.title: Warshall's Algorithm Steps
mnemonics.content: Warshall's: For Every Intermediate (k), check if path through k improves reachability. Use 3 nested loops: k→i→j.

mnemonics.title: Boolean Multiplication
mnemonics.content: BOOL-M: Bring Out And - combine (AND), get result (OR). A∧B must be true for any true result.

mnemonics.title: Strong vs Weak Connection
mnemonics.content: STRONG needs direction both ways (←→), WEAK ignores direction (->). Think: Strong = Street with two-way traffic.

mnemonics.title: Adjacency Matrix Row/Column
mnemonics.content: Row = Roads OUT (outdegree), Column = Cars IN (indegree). Row goes out, column comes in.

mnemonics.title: Transitive Closure Meaning
mnemonics.content: Transitive: If A→B and B→C, then A→C. CLOSURE: Add all missing edges that transitivity demands.

mnemonics.title: Incidence Matrix Values
mnemonics.content: TAIL (-1) starts negative and heads to positive (+1). Negative tail, positive head.

acronyms.usage: Checking if destination is reachable from source in digraph

acronyms.expansion: Adjacency - is there direct edge from i to j?
acronyms.usage: Representing graph connections in matrix form

acronyms.expansion: We Can Honestly Reach All Long Links - algorithm name

visualTips.visualization: Draw vertex set vertically on left, horizontally on top. Fill cell (i,j) = 1 if edge i→j exists. Think of it as a lookup table: row=from, column=to.
Use 3D thinking: n layers (k), each n×n matrix showing paths using vertices up to k as intermediate. Layer by layer, bubbles grow showing more reachability.
Think of row × column as 'any common 1': Row i has {vertices reachable in 1 step}; Column j has {vertices that can reach j in 1 step}. Intersection = paths of length 2.
Start with adjacency matrix (paths of length 1). After Warshall's step k, include all paths using intermediate vertices {1,2,...,k}. Visualize 'bubbles' of reachability expanding.
Edges as columns (vertical slices). Each edge column has exactly two 1s (or -1 and 1 for digraph). Vertex-row tells which edges touch that vertex.

analogies.analogy: Like a seating chart: row = person asking 'Who do I know?', column = person being asked 'Who knows me?'.
Like 'friend of friend' extension: If A knows B and B knows C, transitive closure adds A knows C even if they never met directly.
Like building a flight map: Start with direct flights. Then add connections through intermediate airports. Finally, your map shows all possible destinations.
Like planning a party: You need at least one common friend (AND) to connect two groups (OR). Find if there's any connecting person between two groups.
Strong = telephone network (can call both ways). Weak = one-way roads (ignoring direction, can you still get anywhere?).
